content.thinking: Let me start by exploring the unsloth codebase to understand how it's structured and how existing save/export methods work.

input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase to understand its structure. I need to find:
1. The main directory structure
2. Files related to saving/exporting models (look for save, export, gguf, adapter keywords)
3. How LoRA adapters are saved
4. How GGUF export works
5. How merged model saving works

Start by listing the top-level directory, then explore relevant subdirectories. Be thorough - look at actual file contents of the save/export related files. Report:
- Full directory structure
- Names and paths of all save/export related files
- Key classes and methods in those files (with line numbers)
- How LoRA adapter weights are accessed (A and B matrices)
- What base classes or mixins are used
- How new methods are registered/exposed

Thoroughness: very thorough

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable
1045	    quantize_location = get_executable(["llama-quantize", "quantize", "llama-quantize.exe", "quantize.exe"])
1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:
1050	        print("Unsloth: llama.cpp found in the system. We shall skip installation.")
1051	    else:
1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:
1054	            _run_installer, IS_CMAKE = _run_installer
1055	
1056	            error = _run_installer.wait()
1057	            # Check if successful
1058	            if error != 0:
1059	                print(f"Unsloth: llama.cpp error code = {error}.")
1060	                install_llama_cpp_old(-10)
1061	            pass
1062	
1063	            if IS_CMAKE:
1064	                # CMAKE needs to do some extra steps
1065	                print("Unsloth: CMAKE detected. Finalizing some steps for installation.")
1066	
1067	                check = os.system("cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp")
1068	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1069	                check = os.system("rm -rf llama.cpp/build")
1070	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1071	            pass

1769	    self,

1786	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1787	):
1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
1830	
1831	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front

1833	
1834	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
1835	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
1836	
1837	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
1838	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()
1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1842	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1843	            makefile = None
1844	        else:
1845	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1847	            git_clone.wait()

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
233	
234	
235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,
256	    )

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed report of my findings:

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is a complete analysis of the save/export functionality:

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main package initialization
├── save.py                     # Core save/export functionality (2679 lines)
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer handling (41,190 bytes)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template management
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── models/                     # Model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── _utils.py               # Model utilities (68,479 bytes)
│   ├── loader.py               # Model loading (45,041 bytes)
│   ├── loader_utils.py         # Loader utilities
│   ├── mapper.py               # Layer mapping
│   ├── llama.py                # Llama model (131,612 bytes)
│   ├── mistral.py              # Mistral implementation
│   ├── qwen3.py, qwen2.py      # Qwen models
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py     # Gemma models
│   ├── falcon_h1.py            # Falcon implementation
│   ├── cohere.py               # Cohere implementation
│   ├── granite.py              # Granite implementation
│   ├── rl.py                   # RL training (47,092 bytes)
│   ├── rl_replacements.py      # RL replacements
│   ├── vision.py               # Vision models (47,092 bytes)
│   ├── dpo.py                  # DPO training
│   └── llama4.py               # Llama 4 support
├── kernels/                    # Low-level implementations
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── utils.py                # LoRA parameter extraction (28,630 bytes)
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # Fast LoRA kernels (18,743 bytes)
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py   # Loss functions
│   ├── flex_attention.py        # Flex attention
│   ├── geglu.py, swiglu.py     # Activation functions
│   ├── layernorm.py, rms_layernorm.py  # Normalization
│   ├── rope_embedding.py        # RoPE embeddings
│   └── moe/                    # Mixture of Experts
├── registry/                   # Model registries
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registry.py
│   ├── _llama.py, _mistral.py  # Model configs
│   ├── _qwen.py, _gemma.py
│   ├── _phi.py, _deepseek.py
└── dataprep/                   # Data preparation
```

### 2. **Save/Export Related Files**

**Main file:**
- `/unsloth/save.py` - 2679 lines, contains all save/export functionality

**Test files:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main test file for save functionality
- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Various merge and save tests
- `/tests/saving/vision_models/` - Vision model save tests

### 3. **Core Export Functions in save.py**

#### **A. LoRA Adapter Weight Access**

**Lines 159-187: `_merge_lora(layer, name)` function**
```
- Accesses layer.lora_A[adapter].weight (LoRA A matrix)
- Accesses layer.lora_B[adapter].weight (LoRA B matrix)  
- Accesses layer.scaling[adapter] (scaling factor)
- Merges using: W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha = s)
- Handles quantization state via get_lora_parameters_bias()
```

**Lines 190-204: `fast_save_pickle(shard, name)` function**
- Saves weight shards using torch.save()

#### **B. Main Save Function**

**Lines 205-762: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, ...)`**
- Parameters:
  - `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
  - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HuggingFace upload
  - `maximum_memory_usage`: Float (0-0.95) for memory control
  - `temporary_location`: Temp directory for buffers
  
- Key behaviors:
  - **LoRA saving** (lines 321-330): Saves PEFT format adapters only
  - **Merged 4bit** (lines 293-303): Calls `model.merge_and_unload()`
  - **Merged 16bit** (lines 483-730): Merges LoRA into float16
  - Handles HuggingFace token authentication (lines 263-273)
  - Manages memory with `maximum_memory_usage` (line 275)

#### **C. GGUF Export Function**

**Lines 952-1320: `save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, ...)`**
- Parameters:
  - `quantization_method`: List of ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m", etc.]
  - `first_conversion`: Initial precision ["f16", "bf16", "f32", "q8_0"]
  
- Key steps:
  1. **Install llama.cpp** (lines 1044-1075): Non-blocking installation
  2. **Validate quantization** (lines 992-1023): Maps method names
  3. **Convert to GGUF** (lines 1179-1239):
     - Calls `llama.cpp/convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
     - Patches metadata: "quantized_by: Unsloth"
     - Handles sentencepiece tokenizers (line 1187)
  4. **Quantize** (lines 1269-1320): Runs `llama-quantize` for each method

#### **D. Merged Model Saving**

**Lines 1321-1361: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, save_directory, ...)`**
- Method added to model via `patch_saving_functions()`
- Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
- Cleans memory after save (lines 1359-1360)

**Lines 1364-1435: `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(self, repo_id, ...)`**
- Same as above but pushes to HuggingFace Hub

#### **E. GGUF Specific Methods**

**Lines 1768-1950: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, save_directory, ...)`**
- Wraps merged 16bit save with GGUF conversion
- Merges 4bit quantization to float16 first
- Installs llama.cpp if needed
- Calls `save_to_gguf()` internally
- Handles BOS token fixing (line 1832)
- Supports GGML conversion (lines 2129-2210)

**Lines 1951-2128: `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(self, repo_id, ...)`**
- Same as above but for HuggingFace Hub

#### **F. LoRA to GGML Conversion**

**Lines 2129-2140: `save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)`**
- Saves adapters with `save_method="lora"`

**Lines 2143-2210: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub/locally`**
- Converts LoRA to GGML format
- Uses `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`

#### **G. Generic Save (for non-language models)**

**Lines 2356-2410: `unsloth_generic_save(model, tokenizer, ...)`**
- Alternative save path for vision/non-standard models

**Lines 2413-2501: `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(self, ...)`**
- Generic merged save for any model type

### 4. **How LoRA Adapter Weights Are Accessed**

**From `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`, lines 190-256:**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (W, quant_state, A, B, s)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", ("default",))[0]
    
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # LoRA A matrix
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # LoRA B matrix
    s = proj.scaling[adapter]            # Scaling factor
    return W, quant_state, A, B, s

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """Returns (W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias)"""
    # Same as above + returns base_layer.bias
```

**Key access patterns:**
- **A matrix**: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape: [r, in_features])
- **B matrix**: `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape: [out_features, r])
- **Scaling**: `proj.scaling[adapter]` (scalar, default 1.0)
- **Merged operation**: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s`

### 5. **Base Classes and Mixins**

**PEFT integration (from save.py imports):**
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` - 4bit quantized LoRA linear layer
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` - Standard LoRA linear layer
- `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` - BnB 4bit quantization

**Model inheritance chain:**
- `FastLanguageModel` (llama.py, line 1778) - Base class
- `FastLlamaModel` - Llama implementation
- `FastModel` (loader.py, line 526) - Generic model loader
- `FastVisionModel` (loader.py, line 995) - Vision models
- Models get patched via `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`

### 6. **Method Registration and Exposure**

**Lines 2569-2678: `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`**

Registers these methods on the model object:

```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
```

For vision models, limited to:
- `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_merged`
- `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_gguf`
- `save_pretrained_torchao`

**Where `patch_saving_functions()` is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` lines 2188, 2204, 2902 (for tokenizer and model)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` lines 702, 703, 918, 964
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` line 2309

### 7. **Save Method Options**

The framework supports three save modes:

| Mode | Description | Use Case |
|------|-------------|----------|
| **"lora"** | Save only PEFT adapter weights | HuggingFace inference with base model |
| **"merged_16bit"** | Merge LoRA into float16 weights | GGUF conversion, llama.cpp |
| **"merged_4bit"** | Merge LoRA into int4 quantized weights | Final inference, DPO training |

### 8. **GGUF Quantization Methods Supported**

Lines 77-90 define ALLOWED_QUANTS with these quantization levels:
- **f32, f16, bf16** - Full precision formats
- **q8_0** - 8-bit quantization
- **q4_k_m, q4_k_s, q4_0, q4_1** - 4-bit variants
- **q5_k_m, q5_k_s, q5_0, q5_1** - 5-bit variants
- **q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, q2_k** - 3-bit and 2-bit
- **q6_k** - 6-bit quantization
- **iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs** - IQ quantizations

### 9. **Export Function Hierarchy**

```
unsloth_save_model() [universal entry point]
├── LoRA path → PEFT save_pretrained()
├── Merged 4bit path → merge_and_unload() + save_pretrained()
└── Merged 16bit path → _merge_lora() for each layer + save_pretrained()
    └── Can be followed by save_to_gguf()
        ├── convert-hf-to-gguf.py [llama.cpp]
        └── llama-quantize [llama.cpp]
            └── Creates GGUF files (.gguf)
```

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| save.py | 2679 | All save/export logic |
| kernels/utils.py | 28,630B | LoRA param extraction |
| kernels/fast_lora.py | 18,743B | Fast LoRA forward/backward |
| models/llama.py | 131,612B | Llama model implementation |
| models/loader.py | 45,041B | Model loading factory |
| models/_utils.py | 68,479B | Model utilities |

This architecture allows unsloth to provide a unified interface for saving models in multiple formats while abstracting away the complexity of quantization, LoRA merging, and format conversion.

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

Based on a thorough exploration of the unsloth codebase, here is a complete analysis of the save/export functionality:

### 1. **Top-Level Directory Structure**

```
/unsloth/
├── __init__.py                 # Main package initialization
├── save.py                     # Core save/export functionality (2679 lines)
├── trainer.py                  # Training utilities
├── tokenizer_utils.py          # Tokenizer handling (41,190 bytes)
├── chat_templates.py           # Chat template management
├── import_fixes.py             # Import compatibility fixes
├── models/                     # Model implementations
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── _utils.py               # Model utilities (68,479 bytes)
│   ├── loader.py               # Model loading (45,041 bytes)
│   ├── loader_utils.py         # Loader utilities
│   ├── mapper.py               # Layer mapping
│   ├── llama.py                # Llama model (131,612 bytes)
│   ├── mistral.py              # Mistral implementation
│   ├── qwen3.py, qwen2.py      # Qwen models
│   ├── gemma.py, gemma2.py     # Gemma models
│   ├── falcon_h1.py            # Falcon implementation
│   ├── cohere.py               # Cohere implementation
│   ├── granite.py              # Granite implementation
│   ├── rl.py                   # RL training (47,092 bytes)
│   ├── rl_replacements.py      # RL replacements
│   ├── vision.py               # Vision models (47,092 bytes)
│   ├── dpo.py                  # DPO training
│   └── llama4.py               # Llama 4 support
├── kernels/                    # Low-level implementations
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── utils.py                # LoRA parameter extraction (28,630 bytes)
│   ├── fast_lora.py            # Fast LoRA kernels (18,743 bytes)
│   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py   # Loss functions
│   ├── flex_attention.py        # Flex attention
│   ├── geglu.py, swiglu.py     # Activation functions
│   ├── layernorm.py, rms_layernorm.py  # Normalization
│   ├── rope_embedding.py        # RoPE embeddings
│   └── moe/                    # Mixture of Experts
├── registry/                   # Model registries
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── registry.py
│   ├── _llama.py, _mistral.py  # Model configs
│   ├── _qwen.py, _gemma.py
│   ├── _phi.py, _deepseek.py
└── dataprep/                   # Data preparation
```

### 2. **Save/Export Related Files**

**Main file:**
- `/unsloth/save.py` - 2679 lines, contains all save/export functionality

**Test files:**
- `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main test file for save functionality
- `/tests/saving/language_models/` - Various merge and save tests
- `/tests/saving/vision_models/` - Vision model save tests

### 3. **Core Export Functions in save.py**

#### **A. LoRA Adapter Weight Access**

**Lines 159-187: `_merge_lora(layer, name)` function**
```
- Accesses layer.lora_A[adapter].weight (LoRA A matrix)
- Accesses layer.lora_B[adapter].weight (LoRA B matrix)  
- Accesses layer.scaling[adapter] (scaling factor)
- Merges using: W.addmm_(A.t(), B.t(), alpha = s)
- Handles quantization state via get_lora_parameters_bias()
```

**Lines 190-204: `fast_save_pickle(shard, name)` function**
- Saves weight shards using torch.save()

#### **B. Main Save Function**

**Lines 205-762: `unsloth_save_model(model, tokenizer, save_directory, ...)`**
- Parameters:
  - `save_method`: "lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"
  - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HuggingFace upload
  - `maximum_memory_usage`: Float (0-0.95) for memory control
  - `temporary_location`: Temp directory for buffers
  
- Key behaviors:
  - **LoRA saving** (lines 321-330): Saves PEFT format adapters only
  - **Merged 4bit** (lines 293-303): Calls `model.merge_and_unload()`
  - **Merged 16bit** (lines 483-730): Merges LoRA into float16
  - Handles HuggingFace token authentication (lines 263-273)
  - Manages memory with `maximum_memory_usage` (line 275)

#### **C. GGUF Export Function**

**Lines 952-1320: `save_to_gguf(model_type, model_dtype, ...)`**
- Parameters:
  - `quantization_method`: List of ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m", etc.]
  - `first_conversion`: Initial precision ["f16", "bf16", "f32", "q8_0"]
  
- Key steps:
  1. **Install llama.cpp** (lines 1044-1075): Non-blocking installation
  2. **Validate quantization** (lines 992-1023): Maps method names
  3. **Convert to GGUF** (lines 1179-1239):
     - Calls `llama.cpp/convert-hf-to-gguf.py`
     - Patches metadata: "quantized_by: Unsloth"
     - Handles sentencepiece tokenizers (line 1187)
  4. **Quantize** (lines 1269-1320): Runs `llama-quantize` for each method

#### **D. Merged Model Saving**

**Lines 1321-1361: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, save_directory, ...)`**
- Method added to model via `patch_saving_functions()`
- Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
- Cleans memory after save (lines 1359-1360)

**Lines 1364-1435: `unsloth_push_to_hub_merged(self, repo_id, ...)`**
- Same as above but pushes to HuggingFace Hub

#### **E. GGUF Specific Methods**

**Lines 1768-1950: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, save_directory, ...)`**
- Wraps merged 16bit save with GGUF conversion
- Merges 4bit quantization to float16 first
- Installs llama.cpp if needed
- Calls `save_to_gguf()` internally
- Handles BOS token fixing (line 1832)
- Supports GGML conversion (lines 2129-2210)

**Lines 1951-2128: `unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(self, repo_id, ...)`**
- Same as above but for HuggingFace Hub

#### **F. LoRA to GGML Conversion**

**Lines 2129-2140: `save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory)`**
- Saves adapters with `save_method="lora"`

**Lines 2143-2210: `unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub/locally`**
- Converts LoRA to GGML format
- Uses `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`

#### **G. Generic Save (for non-language models)**

**Lines 2356-2410: `unsloth_generic_save(model, tokenizer, ...)`**
- Alternative save path for vision/non-standard models

**Lines 2413-2501: `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(self, ...)`**
- Generic merged save for any model type

### 4. **How LoRA Adapter Weights Are Accessed**

**From `/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`, lines 190-256:**

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """Returns (W, quant_state, A, B, s)"""
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
    W = base_layer.weight
    
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", ("default",))[0]
    
    A = proj.lora_A[adapter].weight      # LoRA A matrix
    B = proj.lora_B[adapter].weight      # LoRA B matrix
    s = proj.scaling[adapter]            # Scaling factor
    return W, quant_state, A, B, s

def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    """Returns (W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias)"""
    # Same as above + returns base_layer.bias
```

**Key access patterns:**
- **A matrix**: `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` (shape: [r, in_features])
- **B matrix**: `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` (shape: [out_features, r])
- **Scaling**: `proj.scaling[adapter]` (scalar, default 1.0)
- **Merged operation**: `W = W + (A.T @ B.T) * s`

### 5. **Base Classes and Mixins**

**PEFT integration (from save.py imports):**
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear4bit` - 4bit quantized LoRA linear layer
- `peft.tuners.lora.Linear` - Standard LoRA linear layer
- `bitsandbytes.nn.Linear4bit` - BnB 4bit quantization

**Model inheritance chain:**
- `FastLanguageModel` (llama.py, line 1778) - Base class
- `FastLlamaModel` - Llama implementation
- `FastModel` (loader.py, line 526) - Generic model loader
- `FastVisionModel` (loader.py, line 995) - Vision models
- Models get patched via `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`

### 6. **Method Registration and Exposure**

**Lines 2569-2678: `patch_saving_functions(model, vision=False)`**

Registers these methods on the model object:

```python
model.push_to_hub_merged = unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged
model.save_pretrained_merged = unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged
model.push_to_hub_gguf = unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf
model.save_pretrained_gguf = unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf
model.save_pretrained_torchao = unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao
model.push_to_hub_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub
model.save_pretrained_ggml = unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally
```

For vision models, limited to:
- `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_merged`
- `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_gguf`
- `save_pretrained_torchao`

**Where `patch_saving_functions()` is called:**
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` lines 2188, 2204, 2902 (for tokenizer and model)
- `/unsloth/models/vision.py` lines 702, 703, 918, 964
- `/unsloth/chat_templates.py` line 2309

### 7. **Save Method Options**

The framework supports three save modes:

| Mode | Description | Use Case |
|------|-------------|----------|
| **"lora"** | Save only PEFT adapter weights | HuggingFace inference with base model |
| **"merged_16bit"** | Merge LoRA into float16 weights | GGUF conversion, llama.cpp |
| **"merged_4bit"** | Merge LoRA into int4 quantized weights | Final inference, DPO training |

### 8. **GGUF Quantization Methods Supported**

Lines 77-90 define ALLOWED_QUANTS with these quantization levels:
- **f32, f16, bf16** - Full precision formats
- **q8_0** - 8-bit quantization
- **q4_k_m, q4_k_s, q4_0, q4_1** - 4-bit variants
- **q5_k_m, q5_k_s, q5_0, q5_1** - 5-bit variants
- **q3_k_l, q3_k_m, q3_k_s, q2_k** - 3-bit and 2-bit
- **q6_k** - 6-bit quantization
- **iq2_xxs, iq2_xs, iq3_xxs** - IQ quantizations

### 9. **Export Function Hierarchy**

```
unsloth_save_model() [universal entry point]
├── LoRA path → PEFT save_pretrained()
├── Merged 4bit path → merge_and_unload() + save_pretrained()
└── Merged 16bit path → _merge_lora() for each layer + save_pretrained()
    └── Can be followed by save_to_gguf()
        ├── convert-hf-to-gguf.py [llama.cpp]
        └── llama-quantize [llama.cpp]
            └── Creates GGUF files (.gguf)
```

### 10. **Key Files Summary**

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| save.py | 2679 | All save/export logic |
| kernels/utils.py | 28,630B | LoRA param extraction |
| kernels/fast_lora.py | 18,743B | Fast LoRA forward/backward |
| models/llama.py | 131,612B | Llama model implementation |
| models/loader.py | 45,041B | Model loading factory |
| models/_utils.py | 68,479B | Model utilities |

This architecture allows unsloth to provide a unified interface for saving models in multiple formats while abstracting away the complexity of quantization, LoRA merging, and format conversion.

158	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
159	
160	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
161	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
162	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
163	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
164	        if quant_state is not None:
165	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
166	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
167	        else:
168	            dtype = W.dtype
169	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
170	        # W = W.t()
171	
172	        if A is not None:
173	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
174	            # W += sAB
175	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
176	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
177	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
178	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
179	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
180	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
181	        pass
182	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
183	    else:
184	        W = layer.weight
185	    return W, bias

from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import dtype_from_config, HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear as Peft_Linear
from typing import Optional, Callable, Union, List
import sys
import requests
import torch
import os
import shutil
import pickle
import gc
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
import subprocess
import psutil
import re
from transformers.models.llama.modeling_llama import logger
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi
try:
    from huggingface_hub import get_token
except:
    try:
        from huggingface_hub.utils import get_token
    except:
        # For older versions of huggingface_hub
        from huggingface_hub.utils._token import get_token
    pass
pass
from pathlib import Path

__all__ = [
    "print_quantization_methods",
    "unsloth_save_model",
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())
IS_COLAB_ENVIRONMENT  = "\nCOLAB_"  in keynames
IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT = "\nKAGGLE_" in keynames
KAGGLE_TMP = "/tmp"
del keynames

# Weights
LLAMA_WEIGHTS = (
    "self_attn.q_proj", "self_attn.k_proj", "self_attn.v_proj", "self_attn.o_proj",
    "mlp.gate_proj", "mlp.up_proj", "mlp.down_proj",
)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (
    "input_layernorm", "post_attention_layernorm",
    "pre_feedforward_layernorm", "post_feedforward_layernorm",
    "self_attn.q_norm", "self_attn.k_norm",
)

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
    "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
    "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
    "bf16"    : "Bfloat16 - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "f16"     : "Float16  - Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
    "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
    "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
    "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
    # "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization", # Not supported sadly
    # "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
    # "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
    "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
}

def print_quantization_methods():
    for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass
pass


def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model
pass


def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
pass

    push_to_hub          : bool = False,

def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally(
    self,
    save_directory: str, # Added parameter for the folder name
    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    # Use the provided save_directory for local saving
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, save_directory)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(save_directory, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {save_directory} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {save_directory} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """

    del arguments["self"]

def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

def print_quantization_methods():

        print(f'"{key}"  ==> {value}')
    pass

def check_if_sentencepiece_model(model, temporary_location = "_unsloth_sentencepiece_temp"):
    if not hasattr(model, "_saved_temp_tokenizer"): return False

    temp_tokenizer = model._saved_temp_tokenizer
    sentencepiece_model = False
    file_location = os.path.join(temporary_location, temp_tokenizer.name_or_path)
    created_folder = False
    if not os.path.exists(file_location):
        created_folder = True
        os.makedirs(file_location)
    pass
    temp_tokenizer.save_pretrained(file_location)
    if os.path.isfile(f"{file_location}/tokenizer.model"):
        sentencepiece_model = True
    pass
    if created_folder:
        shutil.rmtree(file_location, ignore_errors = True)
    return sentencepiece_model
pass


def _free_cached_model(model):
    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)

    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(
                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
    pass
pass


def _merge_lora(layer, name):

    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
        if quant_state is not None:
            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
        else:
            dtype = W.dtype
        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
        # W = W.t()

        if A is not None:
            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
            # W += sAB
            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
        pass
        W = W.t().to(dtype)
    else:
        W = layer.weight
    return W, bias
pass


def fast_save_pickle(shard, name):
    # Use this if # CPUs is <= 2
    print(f"Unsloth: Saving {name}...")
    torch.save(
        shard,
        name,
        # HIGHEST_PROTOCOL seems to not work with Pytorch!
        # pickle_module   = pickle,
        # pickle_protocol = pickle.HIGHEST_PROTOCOL,
    )
    return
